purpose: C4-A operational artifact: hashable Cut-1 known-vacuous fixture manifest. The real `structural-failure-p2-harness.mjs` objects whose collective behavior the derived C4 audit must classify `MACHINERY_LIVE_ROUTE_TEST_VACUOUS`. C4-B is the two-sided self-test: same audit on this fixture must return vacuous, on the minimal-flip fixture must return non-vacuous.
source_file_immutability: The Cut-1 fixture is fixed real artifact (C4-A §6 [G]). The named objects below MUST NOT be edited; doing so voids the C4-B self-test by changing the known-vacuous side of the two-sided audit. Any modification within these line ranges requires an append-only redesign of C4-B, not a manifest amendment.

fixture_objects.name: makeBundle
fixture_objects.role: C4-B fixture: bundle generator. Produces f_par = R22/cos(h) — the source of Cut-1's g(h) tautology. The thing that, paired with `routeEstimate`/`analyticInverseEstimate`, makes route ≡ analytic baseline by construction.

fixture_objects.name: transparentAdapter
fixture_objects.role: C4-B fixture: the A1 transparent adapter. Inputs `{f_par, f_cza, f_tan, R22, q}`; abstains on L1-ineligible bundles; emits objective `J = -|f_par - R22/cos(q)|`. Decoys are not inputs (structural exclusion — Cut-1 vacuity factor #2).

fixture_objects.name: routeEstimate
fixture_objects.role: C4-B fixture: the Cut-1 route (grid-search extremum seeker on `J`). Inverts `f_par = R22/cos(h)` by grid search — `g^-1(g(h))`. C4 D1 must show that route construction differs from true `h` on the must-differ band; for this fixture it does not (the famous Cut-1 vacuity).

fixture_objects.name: analyticInverseEstimate
fixture_objects.role: C4-B fixture: matched closed-form baseline `q = arccos(R22 / f_par)`. By design identical to the route on the eligible set — this is the C2-B/D1 'route ≡ analytic baseline' equality that motivates D1 comparing route vs TRUE `h`, not vs this baseline.

fixture_objects.name: positiveControlEstimate
fixture_objects.role: C4-B fixture: the decoy-correlate positive control (raw-bundle least-squares over all 8 features). Did move under decoy edits in Cut-1, yielding `OPAQUE_CORRELATE_POSITIVE_CONTROL_CONFIRMED` — the result whose validity stands even after Cut-1 was reclassified vacuous.

fixture_objects.name: routeConstructionAudit
fixture_objects.role: C4-B fixture: the HARDCODED Cut-1 vacuity audit. Returns `{routeTestVacuous: true, ...}` as constants — *asserted*, not *derived* from live objects. The thing C4 reimplements as a computed predicate set; this fixture is the negative side of the C4-B two-sided self-test.

fixture_objects.name: classifyRouteOutcome
fixture_objects.role: C4-B fixture: Cut-1 verdict classifier. Wraps the audit in the verdict tree (MACHINERY_LIVE_ROUTE_TEST_VACUOUS / CONVERGENCE_NULL / INCONCLUSIVE_DECOY_BATTERY / OPAQUE_CORRELATE / ROUTE_NOT_THE_INVERSE / TRACEABILITY_HARNESS_PASS). For C4-B audit replay, this is how the verdict actually surfaces — and why a hardcoded `routeTestVacuous: true` short-circuits to the vacuous verdict regardless of q1/q2/q3.
fixture_objects.first_line_marker: function classifyRouteOutcome({ q1, q2, q3, audit, positiveControlMoved }) {

fixture_vacuity_summary.summary: Cut-1 is g^-1(g(h)) by grid search with a hardcoded vacuity audit; the positive control moves (correlate path exists) but the route side cannot fail the structural test by construction. This is exactly the case the derived C4 audit must catch.
